# @zakkster/lite-headless / tour

Multi-step coach mark / product-tour primitive. State machine + ARIA
wiring + keyboard nav for sequential popovers anchored to UI targets.
Doesn't own positioning (consumer or compose with `createPopover`)
and doesn't persist completion (consumer's call whether to remember).

## What you get

```js
import { createTour } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/tour";

const tour = createTour({
    loop: false,                      // default
    onStepChange: (idx, step) => {},  // fires on every transition
    onComplete:   ()           => {}, // fires when next() past the last step
    onSkip:       (atIdx)      => {}, // fires when skip() is called
});

tour.addStep({ id: "nav", target: document.querySelector("#nav") });
tour.addStep({ id: "new", target: document.querySelector("#new-btn") });

tour.attachRoot(rootEl);
tour.attachStepContent("nav", navStepContentEl);
tour.attachStepContent("new", newStepContentEl);

tour.start();
```

## Step shape

```js
{
    id:     "nav",                  // required (or generated)
    target: navEl,                  // anchor element (optional)
    title:  "Navigation",           // free-form; consumer decides display
    description: "Switch sections", // free-form
}
```

## Reads

- `tour.current()` — current index, -1 if not active
- `tour.currentStep()` — current step object, null if not active
- `tour.steps()` — array of step descriptors
- `tour.count()` — total number of steps
- `tour.isActive()` — true while a step is showing
- `tour.isFirst()` / `tour.isLast()` — boundary checks

## Navigation

- `tour.start()` — show step 0
- `tour.next()` — advance; past the last step calls `finish()`
- `tour.prev()` — go back; at step 0 is no-op (or wraps with `loop: true`)
- `tour.goTo(idxOrId)` — jump to a specific step
- `tour.skip()` — dismiss the tour; fires `onSkip(atIdx)`
- `tour.finish()` — mark complete; fires `onComplete`

## Painted attributes

Root (`[data-tour-root]`):
- `data-tour-active` — boolean; present while a step is showing

Current target (whatever element a step's `target` points at):
- `data-tour-target` — boolean; present only on the active step's target
- `aria-describedby` — points at the active step's content id

Step content (each element passed to `attachStepContent`):
- `role="region"` (only if no role pre-set)
- `data-tour-step="<id>"`
- `data-tour-active` — boolean; present only on the active step
- `tabindex="-1"` — focusable programmatically (the primitive moves focus here on step change)
- `hidden` — set on inactive steps; removed from the active one

## Keyboard

While the tour is active, a global `keydown` handler intercepts:

- `Escape` → `skip()`
- `ArrowRight` → `next()`
- `ArrowLeft` → `prev()`

The handler is bound on `attachRoot` and unbound on `destroy()`. It
listens on `window` so the keys work regardless of which element has
focus.

## Wrapper (`<lite-tour>`)

```html
<lite-tour id="t1" start-on-mount>
    <div data-tour-step="nav" data-tour-target="#nav">
        <h3>Navigation</h3>
        <p>Use this to switch between sections.</p>
        <button data-tour-prev>Back</button>
        <button data-tour-next>Next</button>
        <button data-tour-skip>Skip</button>
    </div>
    <div data-tour-step="new" data-tour-target="#new-btn">
        <h3>Create</h3>
        <p>Click here to start a new project.</p>
        <button data-tour-prev>Back</button>
        <button data-tour-next>Finish</button>
    </div>
</lite-tour>
```

Attributes:
- `start-on-mount` — auto-call `start()` one frame after connect
- `loop` — wrap around when `prev()` at step 0

Slot markers in step content:
- `data-tour-next` / `data-tour-prev` / `data-tour-skip` / `data-tour-finish`
  — any element with these is wired via event delegation; click them
  to navigate

Host accessors:
- `host.index` — current index
- `host.isActive`, `host.isFirst`, `host.isLast`
- `host.count`
- `host.start()`, `host.next()`, `host.prev()`, `host.skip()`, `host.finish()`, `host.goTo(idxOrId)`
- `host._tourInstance` (advanced)

Events:
- `stepchange` — `{ detail: { index, id, target } }`
- `complete`
- `skip` — `{ detail: { atIndex } }`

## Positioning the step content

The primitive doesn't position; the consumer does. Easiest pattern:

```js
tour.attachStepContent("nav", navContentEl);
host.addEventListener("stepchange", e => {
    const target = e.detail.target;
    if (!target) return;
    const r = target.getBoundingClientRect();
    navContentEl.style.left = r.left + "px";
    navContentEl.style.top  = (r.bottom + 8) + "px";
});
```

Or compose with `createPopover` per step for proper Floating UI math.

## Backdrop / spotlight

The primitive doesn't render a backdrop, but consumers can:

```css
[data-tour-active] .tour-backdrop {
    position: fixed; inset: 0;
    background: rgba(0,0,0,0.6);
    pointer-events: none;
}
[data-tour-target] {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 9999;
    box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px var(--accent), 0 0 0 9999px rgba(0,0,0,0.6);
}
```

The `box-shadow` cutout trick gives a spotlight effect without needing
SVG masks.

## CSS contract

Follows the canonical CSS contract in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`.
`data-tour-active`, `data-tour-target` are Class-2 booleans; slot
markers (`data-tour-step`, `data-tour-next`, etc.) are Class-4 inputs.
